sourceScope.primarySourceFile: AG 23 - Guideline Concerning Variable Life Insurance Separate Account Investments.pdf
sourceScope.sourceTitle: AG 23 - Guideline Concerning Variable Life Insurance Separate Account Investments

observedSectionWindows.note: Title, sufficiency standard, definition of readily marketable assets, and the 15% / 10% demonstration thresholds.

boundaries: Keep planning focused on this single AG 23 PDF.
Do not widen into AG 24 or later guideline files.
Keep the active guideline posture explicit in every batch artifact.
Keep the page image as the backstop for exact wording because the text layer is noisy / encoded and includes a placeholder statutory reference.
Treat the guideline as review-only regulatory guidance, not learner-facing policy text.
Keep the batch source-bound and review-only.
exclusions: No eLearning scripts, slides, quizzes, or app exports.
No raw source files in Git.
No other guideline files in the same wave.

topicMap.label: Variable life insurance separate account investments
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep this slice within page 1 and do not absorb later guideline files.

proposedBatches.title: One-page AG 23 separate-account investments guideline

pageTarget.notes: Capture the full one-page guideline as a single review-only slice and stop at the page boundary.

proposedBatches.rationale: Capture the full one-page guideline as a single review-only slice and stop at the page boundary.

reviewStandards.regulatoryRequirement: Treat express duty, permitted treatment, or required separate-account-investment language in the variable-life context as the requirement; preserve the citation and source context.
reviewStandards.definitionOrTerminology: Treat readily marketable assets, net investment income, market value, and the 15% / 10% demonstration thresholds as terminology when the text explains rather than directs.
reviewStandards.reserveMethodStructure: Treat the sufficiency demonstration and the 15% / 10% thresholds as structure until the text clearly instructs a separate duty.
reviewStandards.calculationStructure: Treat the way the guideline frames the sufficiency demonstration as calculation structure until the text clearly instructs a separate duty.
reviewStandards.formulaContext: Treat the asset / obligation comparison and demonstration language as context until the text clearly instructs a calculation step.
reviewStandards.crossReferenceMapping: Treat references to state statutory provisions, separate series / portfolio language, or investment subdivisions as mapping notes unless operational text is included.
reviewStandards.backgroundContent: Treat the title line and framing sentence as background content unless they impose a duty.
reviewStandards.boundaryControlWindow: Treat the one-batch page window as a review-only boundary that should not be merged forward.
reviewStandards.requiresHumanInterpretation: Treat the indexing choice, the noisy OCR layer, and the placeholder statutory reference as requiring human actuarial interpretation unless the text is explicit.
reviewStandards.sourceQualityCaveat: Treat the page-image wording as the backstop and do not widen the batch because OCR looks uncertain.

appReady: export is sanitized, stable, and versioned
ragReady: review status no longer requires human disposition

currentChecksNeeded: Plan file exists and parses as JSON.
The AG 23 plan markdown exists and carries the expected headings.
Every proposed batch is review-only by default.
The plan keeps the scope to page 1 only.
The source posture is explicitly active.
Each planned batch has a matching batch definition.
The AG 23 batch definition file stays synchronized with the plan.
futureChecksSuggested: One-page / short-guideline checks for future compact guideline files.
No learner-facing, app-ready, or RAG-ready promotion checks.
Source-bound separate-account-investment checks once extraction starts.
scriptImplications: scripts/ag23-batch-definitions.mjs should stay synchronized with the planned batch-101 slice when extraction is authorized.
scripts/batch-definitions.mjs should import the AG 23 definition file so the shared runner can find the planned batch ID.
scripts/validate-scaffold.mjs should verify that the AG 23 plan file exists, parses cleanly, and preserves the review-only posture.
The shared runner should continue to honor the same stop conditions used for the earlier guideline batches.

notes: Controlled AG 23 planning artifact. Planned batch 101 remains review-only by default and is not an extraction run.
